input.new_string:
### 7. Sign-out

`POST /auth/signout` clears the Supabase session cookies server-side (via the same `@supabase/ssr` cookie adapter the callback uses) and redirects to `/`. The button is a `<form action="/auth/signout" method="post">` — POST avoids accidental sign-outs from URL prefetchers, and clearing cookies server-side avoids the "client says signed out but SSR pages still think they're signed in" gap.

### 8. Bilingual claim layer (China team)

One unified knowledge graph, rendered per reader language. Design + resolved decisions live in `china_imp.md`; the code is the source of truth.

- **Storage.** A claim stays canonical in its source language (`claims.source_lang`, default `'en'`, stamped at promotion from the authoring employee's `employees.locale`). `claim_translations` `(claim_id, lang)` holds display-only summary renderings, each with its own `embedding` (same `text-embedding-3-small`/1536 model) and a `source_hash` for staleness. Evidence quotes and the synthesized Brain are **never** translated.
- **Retrieval.** `searchWithRetrievalPlan(db, plan, locale)` renders `COALESCE(claim_translations.summary, claims.summary)` and `COALESCE(ct.embedding, c.embedding)` for the reader's locale, and switches the tsvector config to `'simple'` for `zh-CN` (Postgres can't tokenize spaceless Chinese; the vector half of the RRF carries ranking). The locale fragments live in `buildPlanMetadataFilters()` so the parity guard forces both retrieval branches to match. `getBrainSectionSnippets` has no locale path (Brain is English-only).
- **Translation is opt-in.** Admins select claims in Admin → Claims and "Translate selected for China team" (`translateClaimsForChina`) → the `claim-translation` worker drafts via the `translation` auxiliary model (`default_translation_route`), embeds, and upserts `claim_translations`. Untranslated claims are still visible to China readers, in English.
- **"Ask to verify" (recertification).** `requestClaimVerification` → the `claim-recertification` worker fans targets (the `zh-CN` locale group, departments via `employee_departments`, and/or individual employees) out to recipient employees and creates one `gaps` row per recipient (`gap_type='claim_recertification'`, reusing the existing gap→chat surfacing), each question drafted in that recipient's language — so a question is translated to Chinese only for China-team recipients.

## Major constraints

input.new_string:
# HANDOFF — Recall.ai wiring + extraction tuning + China bilingual layer

Last updated: 2026-06-18. Delete this file once the remaining items below are closed (China bilingual merge/deploy, synthesis demo, entity-registry seeding, and any intentional uncommitted local changes are committed/deployed or discarded by the owner).

---

## China bilingual claim layer — implemented on a branch, migration applied, NOT merged/deployed (2026-06-18)

What it is: serve the knowledge graph to a China team in Mandarin while keeping one unified brain. Full design + resolved decisions are in `china_imp.md`; AGENTS.md §7–§10 document the code surfaces.

Exact current state:
- All code is on branch **`docs/china-bilingual-plan`** (GitHub **PR #1**, `u2giants/theoracle`). It is **NOT merged to `main`**, so Vercel production (which deploys from `main`) does not run it yet, and the new workers are **NOT deployed** to Trigger.dev prod.
- DB migration **`0007_tricky_charles_xavier.sql`** WAS applied to the prod DB (`vokucjpanhvqunimlvsp`) via `pnpm db:migrate` on 2026-06-18. Verified live: `claim_translations` table, `claims.source_lang`, `employees.locale`, the `lang` + two FTS indexes. The HNSW vector index on `claim_translations.embedding` was intentionally skipped (build later with `ORACLE_RUN_VECTOR_INDEXES=1` once there is translation data). The additive migration being ahead of the merged code is safe.
- `0007` was hand-trimmed to only the new objects because `drizzle-kit generate` re-emits pre-existing hand-SQL tables (snapshot drift) — see AGENTS.md §10 "The Drizzle snapshot was baselined at migration 0007".

What is done: schema + migration; `SUPPORTED_LOCALES` in `@oracle/shared`; locale-aware `searchWithRetrievalPlan`/`buildPlanMetadataFilters` (+ extended parity guard); `source_lang` stamping at promotion; `claim-translation` worker + `translation` auxiliary model (`default_translation_route`, "copy job brief" in settings); opt-in translate + multi-target "ask to verify" (`claim-recertification` worker reusing the `gaps` table) with per-recipient-language drafting; admin claims UI (checkboxes, two bulk actions, ✓/🔁 persisted badges). All packages typecheck; `verify:retrieval-filter-parity` and `verify:auxiliary-defaults` pass.

Exact next actions:
1. Review/merge PR #1 to `main` (Albert's call — single-branch policy; push to `main` only when he says). Vercel auto-deploys web on merge.
2. Deploy workers: `pnpm --filter @oracle/workers run deploy` (ships `claim-translation` + `claim-recertification`; record the new Trigger.dev worker version here).
3. Set a China employee's `employees.locale='zh-CN'`; choose a translation model at Admin → Settings → "Translation model" (Qwen recommended — run a small A/B vs DeepSeek per the copied brief).
4. Optionally build the discussed-but-not-built follow-ups (see AGENTS.md §15): admin side-by-side translation review; a free-form "compose a gap and send to targets" surface.

Decisions made (and why): Brain synthesis stays English-only; evidence quotes are never translated (verbatim provenance); translation is opt-in per claim (cost proportional to what's directed to China); recertification questions are drafted per recipient so only China recipients get Chinese. Department targeting resolves via the `employee_departments` junction (exact), avoiding the free-text `employees.departments` matching pitfall.

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# The Oracle

The Oracle is an evidence-backed enterprise knowledge graph for POP Creations / Spruce Line. Employees talk to it in chat and upload documents; workers extract operational claims with quote-level evidence; deterministic validators gate promotion; synthesis workers maintain Brain sections; admin screens review the resulting knowledge and AI runs.

Start here:

- `AGENTS.md` — primary developer and AI-session operating guide
- `HANDOFF.md` — active unfinished work, when present
- `docs/architecture.md` — current system design and data flow
- `docs/development.md` — local setup and verification workflow
- `docs/configuration.md` — env vars and runtime config
- `docs/deployment.md` — release/deploy process
- `DECISIONS.md` — architectural decisions and constraints
- `oracle_master_spec.md` — product contract

## Repo map

- `apps/web/` — Next.js 16 web app, admin UI, chat route, Teams transcript webhook (`/api/teams/notifications`), Recall live Teams webhook (`/api/teams/live/recall`)
- `apps/workers/` — Trigger.dev workers (claim extraction, doc ingestion, synthesis, contradiction watcher, Teams transcript ingestion + subscription manager, Recall live Teams utterance processor)
- `packages/ai/` — `OracleAIClient`, retrieval, prompts, provider adapters
- `packages/db/` — Drizzle schema, migrations, seed, DB client
- `packages/oracle-engines/` — deterministic validation/promotion/synthesis logic
- `packages/auth/` — auth linking helpers
- `packages/shared/` — shared types/constants

## Current runtime shape

- Web app: Vercel via `vercel.json`
- Workers: Trigger.dev Cloud via `apps/workers/trigger.config.ts`
- Database/Auth/Storage/Realtime: Supabase Cloud
- AI inference: direct provider adapters only
  - Anthropic via `@anthropic-ai/sdk`
  - Vertex via `@google/genai`
  - OpenAI via `openai`
  - DeepSeek via `openai` against `api.deepseek.com`
  - Qwen via `openai` against DashScope OpenAI-compatible endpoints
- OpenRouter is enrichment-only for the model catalog, not inference

## Fast start

```bash
pnpm install
pnpm typecheck
pnpm --filter @oracle/web build
pnpm dev
```

For a real local setup you also need `.env.local`, Supabase access, and Vertex ADC. Use `docs/development.md` and `docs/configuration.md` for the exact steps.

## Notes

- `HANDOFF.md` should exist only when work is unfinished. If it is missing, there is no active handoff file.
- `AGENTS.md` is the canonical guide for future sessions. Keep it current when behavior or operations change.

content.text:
Stop — I need to flag something before touching `main`, because this is now a production-affecting merge, not the clean landing it looked like.

## The situation

- `origin/main` is **31 commits ahead** of where this branch forked. Those commits are substantial and land in the **same files** my China work touched — notably a reworked claims admin flow ("Add claim revision workflow", "Add claim review groups", "employee access controls", "Prioritize directly assigned claims") and model-routing changes ("Support dynamic model routes in router").
- **12 files conflict** (both sides edited them): `schema.ts`, `retrieval.ts`, `apps/web/app/admin/claims/{page,_actions}.tsx`, `settings/page.tsx`, `chat/route.ts`, `packages/ai/src/index.ts`, `promotion-executor.ts`, `domains.ts`, plus the three docs.
- **Good news:** no migration-number collision — `origin/main` only goes up to `0006`, so my `0007_tricky_charles_xavier.sql` slots in cleanly. (The `0007_snapshot.json` will still need a sanity check, since it was generated without main's 31 commits of schema edits.)

So "commit everything to main and push" requires a real **merge with conflict resolution across 12 files**, and pushing the result auto-deploys the web app to production. A careless resolution — especially in the claims admin page, where main added a review/revision workflow and I added the translate/"ask to verify" bulk actions to the same UI — could compile but be semantically wrong in production.

## How I recommend proceeding

I'd do this safely rather than blind-merge:

1. Merge `origin/main` into the branch and resolve all 12 conflicts (carefully composing the two claims-admin feature sets, not clobbering either).
2. Re-run `pnpm -r typecheck`, `verify:retrieval-filter-parity`, `verify:auxiliary-defaults`, and the web build.
3. Re-check the `0007` snapshot against the merged `schema.ts`.
4. Only then fast-forward `main` and push (→ Vercel prod deploy). Workers still need a separate `pnpm --filter @oracle/workers run deploy` afterward.

I haven't changed any git state yet. Two questions before I push to production:

content.text:
Merge aborted — branch is clean, nothing pushed, no damage. Here's what the merge surfaced, and why I stopped.

## The real conflict isn't mechanical — it's a feature overlap

8 of the 12 files auto-merged fine. Of the 4 conflicts:
- **`schema.ts`** and **`HANDOFF.md`** are mechanical (both sides added different things — easy).
- **`apps/web/app/admin/claims/_actions.ts`** and **`page.tsx`** are the problem. While this branch was open, `main` shipped a **full claim-review system** on the *same* admin page: approve/reject/**revise**/assign, a reviewer-permission model (`requireClaimReviewer`, non-admin assigned reviewers), `claim_review_events`, and — critically — **claim review groups** (`/admin/claim-groups`, `claim_review_groups`, migrations `67`–`73`) with a feature that **sends a claim-review question to multiple people or reusable groups** via `gap_type='claim_review_question'`.

That last piece is essentially the same thing I just built as "ask to verify" (`claim-recertification`, `gap_type='claim_recertification'`, multi-target send). **My recertification feature substantially duplicates a feature `main` already shipped.** Naively merging would either create two parallel "ask people about a claim" systems or quietly break `main`'s review workflow — exactly the semantically-wrong-but-compiles risk I flagged.

The **translation half** of my work (claim_translations, locale-aware retrieval, translate worker, the bilingual schema) is largely orthogonal and merges cleanly apart from the mechanical `schema.ts` conflict. It's only the **verify/recertification half** that collides.

So I need a product decision before I can resolve this correctly:

questions.question: main already shipped a claim-review-groups feature that sends review questions to people/groups (gap_type='claim_review_question'), which overlaps my 'ask to verify' (claim-recertification). How should I reconcile when merging?

options.label: Fold verify into main's system
options.description: Land only the translation half as net-new. Drop my separate claim-recertification worker/gap_type and re-express 'ask to verify' using main's existing claim_review_question + review-groups mechanism (adding the per-recipient Chinese translation rule there). Least duplication; aligns with main.

options.label: Land translation only, defer verify
options.description: Merge just the bilingual translation feature now (clean). Leave 'ask to verify' out of main entirely until we decide how it relates to main's review-groups feature.

options.label: Keep both separate
options.description: Merge my claim-recertification as-is alongside main's claim_review_question. Fastest, but ships two parallel 'ask about a claim' systems with overlapping gap types — likely confusing later.

content.text:
Now I understand main's `assignClaimQuestion` — it's the more mature mechanism: reusable persisted **review groups** + individuals → one `claim_review_question` gap per recipient, with idempotency, a custom/default question, and review-event audit. What it lacks is my two China-specific contributions: **AI-drafted questions** and **per-recipient Chinese translation** (it sends one English question to everyone).

So the "best of both" is clear: **main's review-question + groups is the canonical mechanism; fold my China value into it** (translate the question per-recipient when the recipient is `zh-CN`), drop my separate `claim_recertification` worker/gap-type, and keep my orthogonal **translation feature**. Let me read main's claims page and schema to integrate cleanly.

7	
8	## China bilingual claim layer — implemented on a branch, migration applied, NOT merged/deployed (2026-06-18)
9	
10	What it is: serve the knowledge graph to a China team in Mandarin while keeping one unified brain. Full design + resolved decisions are in `china_imp.md`; AGENTS.md §7–§10 document the code surfaces.
11	
12	Exact current state:
13	- All code is on branch **`docs/china-bilingual-plan`** (GitHub **PR #1**, `u2giants/theoracle`). It is **NOT merged to `main`**, so Vercel production (which deploys from `main`) does not run it yet, and the new workers are **NOT deployed** to Trigger.dev prod.
14	- DB migration **`0007_tricky_charles_xavier.sql`** WAS applied to the prod DB (`vokucjpanhvqunimlvsp`) via `pnpm db:migrate` on 2026-06-18. Verified live: `claim_translations` table, `claims.source_lang`, `employees.locale`, the `lang` + two FTS indexes. The HNSW vector index on `claim_translations.embedding` was intentionally skipped (build later with `ORACLE_RUN_VECTOR_INDEXES=1` once there is translation data). The additive migration being ahead of the merged code is safe.
15	- `0007` was hand-trimmed to only the new objects because `drizzle-kit generate` re-emits pre-existing hand-SQL tables (snapshot drift) — see AGENTS.md §10 "The Drizzle snapshot was baselined at migration 0007".
16	
17	What is done: schema + migration; `SUPPORTED_LOCALES` in `@oracle/shared`; locale-aware `searchWithRetrievalPlan`/`buildPlanMetadataFilters` (+ extended parity guard); `source_lang` stamping at promotion; `claim-translation` worker + `translation` auxiliary model (`default_translation_route`, "copy job brief" in settings); opt-in translate + multi-target "ask to verify" (`claim-recertification` worker reusing the `gaps` table) with per-recipient-language drafting; admin claims UI (checkboxes, two bulk actions, ✓/🔁 persisted badges). All packages typecheck; `verify:retrieval-filter-parity` and `verify:auxiliary-defaults` pass.
18	
19	Exact next actions:
20	1. Review/merge PR #1 to `main` (Albert's call — single-branch policy; push to `main` only when he says). Vercel auto-deploys web on merge.
21	2. Deploy workers: `pnpm --filter @oracle/workers run deploy` (ships `claim-translation` + `claim-recertification`; record the new Trigger.dev worker version here).
22	3. Set a China employee's `employees.locale='zh-CN'`; choose a translation model at Admin → Settings → "Translation model" (Qwen recommended — run a small A/B vs DeepSeek per the copied brief).
23	4. Optionally build the discussed-but-not-built follow-ups (see AGENTS.md §15): admin side-by-side translation review; a free-form "compose a gap and send to targets" surface.
24	
25	Decisions made (and why): Brain synthesis stays English-only; evidence quotes are never translated (verbatim provenance); translation is opt-in per claim (cost proportional to what's directed to China); recertification questions are drafted per recipient so only China recipients get Chinese. Department targeting resolves via the `employee_departments` junction (exact), avoiding the free-text `employees.departments` matching pitfall.

Migration `72_claim_extraction_ab_run_status.sql` was applied to production through linked Supabase CLI because this checkout did not have `DIRECT_URL` / `DATABASE_URL` for `pnpm db:migrate`.

# HANDOFF — Recall.ai wiring + extraction tuning + China bilingual layer

<<<<<<< HEAD
Last updated: 2026-06-18. Delete this file once the remaining items below are closed (China bilingual merge/deploy, synthesis demo, entity-registry seeding, and any intentional uncommitted local changes are committed/deployed or discarded by the owner).

---

## China bilingual claim layer — implemented on a branch, migration applied, NOT merged/deployed (2026-06-18)

What it is: serve the knowledge graph to a China team in Mandarin while keeping one unified brain. Full design + resolved decisions are in `china_imp.md`; AGENTS.md §7–§10 document the code surfaces.

Exact current state:
- All code is on branch **`docs/china-bilingual-plan`** (GitHub **PR #1**, `u2giants/theoracle`). It is **NOT merged to `main`**, so Vercel production (which deploys from `main`) does not run it yet, and the new workers are **NOT deployed** to Trigger.dev prod.
- DB migration **`0007_tricky_charles_xavier.sql`** WAS applied to the prod DB (`vokucjpanhvqunimlvsp`) via `pnpm db:migrate` on 2026-06-18. Verified live: `claim_translations` table, `claims.source_lang`, `employees.locale`, the `lang` + two FTS indexes. The HNSW vector index on `claim_translations.embedding` was intentionally skipped (build later with `ORACLE_RUN_VECTOR_INDEXES=1` once there is translation data). The additive migration being ahead of the merged code is safe.
- `0007` was hand-trimmed to only the new objects because `drizzle-kit generate` re-emits pre-existing hand-SQL tables (snapshot drift) — see AGENTS.md §10 "The Drizzle snapshot was baselined at migration 0007".

What is done: schema + migration; `SUPPORTED_LOCALES` in `@oracle/shared`; locale-aware `searchWithRetrievalPlan`/`buildPlanMetadataFilters` (+ extended parity guard); `source_lang` stamping at promotion; `claim-translation` worker + `translation` auxiliary model (`default_translation_route`, "copy job brief" in settings); opt-in translate + multi-target "ask to verify" (`claim-recertification` worker reusing the `gaps` table) with per-recipient-language drafting; admin claims UI (checkboxes, two bulk actions, ✓/🔁 persisted badges). All packages typecheck; `verify:retrieval-filter-parity` and `verify:auxiliary-defaults` pass.

Exact next actions:
1. Review/merge PR #1 to `main` (Albert's call — single-branch policy; push to `main` only when he says). Vercel auto-deploys web on merge.
2. Deploy workers: `pnpm --filter @oracle/workers run deploy` (ships `claim-translation` + `claim-recertification`; record the new Trigger.dev worker version here).
3. Set a China employee's `employees.locale='zh-CN'`; choose a translation model at Admin → Settings → "Translation model" (Qwen recommended — run a small A/B vs DeepSeek per the copied brief).
4. Optionally build the discussed-but-not-built follow-ups (see AGENTS.md §15): admin side-by-side translation review; a free-form "compose a gap and send to targets" surface.

Decisions made (and why): Brain synthesis stays English-only; evidence quotes are never translated (verbatim provenance); translation is opt-in per claim (cost proportional to what's directed to China); recertification questions are drafted per recipient so only China recipients get Chinese. Department targeting resolves via the `employee_departments` junction (exact), avoiding the free-text `employees.departments` matching pitfall.
=======

## 2026-06-16 extraction A/B + employee-access update

Status:
Done. Code was committed, pushed, DB changes were applied where needed, and production web/workers were deployed.

Done:
- Fixed `/admin/ai/extraction-ab` source alignment: A/B/C model reruns now anchor to reviewed evidence quotes and do not fall back to unrelated leading document-chunk text when the quote is missing from the chunk.
- Moved extraction A/B/C reruns out of the page server action. `/admin/ai/extraction-ab` now queues rows through `claim_extraction_ab_tests.run_status`; Trigger.dev task `extraction-ab-eval` runs Gemini/Qwen and writes outputs/errors back. Migration `72_claim_extraction_ab_run_status.sql` was applied to production through linked Supabase CLI because this checkout did not have `DIRECT_URL` / `DATABASE_URL` for `pnpm db:migrate`.
- Trigger.dev worker deploy `20260616.2` succeeded after removing the attempted cron sweep. Trigger.dev was at 10/10 schedule slots, so `extraction-ab-eval` uses immediate dispatch only.
- Added Admin -> Employees Disable/Re-enable controls. They write `employees.disabled_at`; disabled employees cannot log in/link, but historical employee references remain intact.
- Production Vercel deployments were completed through commit `39c58fd Add employee access controls` before this documentation pass.

Next action:
Use Admin -> Employees for roster cleanup. For A/B/C evals, click rerun rows and let the queued worker populate results; if rows remain queued indefinitely, verify Vercel production `TRIGGER_SECRET_KEY` is a prod Trigger key and inspect Trigger.dev task runs for `extraction-ab-eval`.

Risks / watchouts:
- Do not hard-delete employees unless a separate archival/reference cleanup is designed.
- Do not move Gemini/Qwen A/B/C calls back into the page server action; multiple simultaneous rows previously hung the UI.
- Do not add another Trigger.dev schedule until the schedule limit is increased or existing schedules are consolidated.

AGENTS.md:41:| China bilingual claim layer / claim translation / "ask to verify" (recertification gaps) | `AGENTS.md` §7–§10, `china_imp.md` (design + resolved decisions), `packages/ai/src/retrieval.ts`, `apps/workers/src/trigger/claim-translation.ts`, `apps/workers/src/trigger/claim-recertification.ts`, `apps/web/app/admin/claims/*` | Teams/Recall docs; provider-adapter internals |

AGENTS.md:223:| Employee locale | `employees.locale` | `packages/db/src/schema.ts` | Reader/content language (`varchar(12)`, default `'en'`). Admin sets `'zh-CN'` to put an employee on the "China team". Drives retrieval rendering, answer language, and recertification-question language. |
AGENTS.md:227:| Claim recertification worker | task `claim-recertification` | `apps/workers/src/trigger/claim-recertification.ts` | "Ask to verify": fans targets (locale group / employees / departments) out to recipient employees and creates one `gaps` row per recipient (`gap_type='claim_recertification'`), drafted in each recipient's language. Triggered by `requestClaimVerification`. |
AGENTS.md:274:- `china_imp.md` unless the task is the China bilingual claim layer / claim translation / recertification
AGENTS.md:826:There is exactly ONE knowledge graph. A claim stays canonical in `claims.source_lang` with its verbatim `claim_evidence.exactQuote` intact; `claim_translations` holds display-only summary renderings per language. `searchWithRetrievalPlan(db, plan, locale)` renders `COALESCE(translation, canonical)` for the reader's locale (and `'simple'` tsvector config for `zh-CN`, since Postgres can't tokenize spaceless Chinese). Three deliberate boundaries: (1) **evidence quotes are never translated** — they must stay byte-for-byte for the quote validator; (2) **Brain synthesis is English-only** — `getBrainSectionSnippets` has no locale path; (3) **translation is opt-in per claim** (admin selects claims to send to the China team) and **recertification questions are drafted per-recipient** so a question is translated to Chinese only when a recipient is a `zh-CN` employee.
AGENTS.md:1047:| blocked | China bilingual claim layer (schema, locale-aware retrieval, translation worker, recertification worker, admin UI) is implemented on branch `docs/china-bilingual-plan` (GitHub PR #1) and migration `0007` is **applied to the prod DB**, but the **code is not merged to `main`** and the new workers are **not deployed to Trigger.dev prod**. Additive migration is safe to have ahead of code. | Review/merge PR #1 to `main` → Vercel auto-deploys web; `pnpm --filter @oracle/workers run deploy` to ship `claim-translation` + `claim-recertification`. Then set a China employee's `locale='zh-CN'` and pick a translation model. See `HANDOFF.md`. |
AGENTS.md:1048:| open | China bilingual follow-ups discussed but not built: backfill of existing approved claims (moot — translation is opt-in), admin side-by-side translation review, and a free-form "compose a gap and send to targets" surface (only the claim-verify path exists). | Build on request; the recipient-resolution + per-language drafting primitive in `claim-recertification.ts` is reusable. |
docs/architecture.md:454:- **"Ask to verify" (recertification).** `requestClaimVerification` → the `claim-recertification` worker fans targets (the `zh-CN` locale group, departments via `employee_departments`, and/or individual employees) out to recipient employees and creates one `gaps` row per recipient (`gap_type='claim_recertification'`, reusing the existing gap→chat surfacing), each question drafted in that recipient's language — so a question is translated to Chinese only for China-team recipients.
china_imp.md:425:| Bulk "ask to verify" (recertification) | Admin selects claims + one or more targets (China-team locale group, departments, and/or individual employees) and asks them to confirm/correct each ("is this still accurate?"). Reuses the `gaps` table (`gap_type='claim_recertification'`) — no schema change; surfaced via the existing gap→chat path. A 🔁 badge shows pending asks (persists across refresh). |
README.md:19:- `apps/workers/` — Trigger.dev workers (claim extraction, doc ingestion, synthesis, contradiction watcher, Teams transcript ingestion + subscription manager, Recall live Teams utterance processor, China bilingual claim translation + recertification)

content.text: Now the §10 quirk wording and §15 pending-work rows:

content.content:
825	Actually:
826	There is exactly ONE knowledge graph. A claim stays canonical in `claims.source_lang` with its verbatim `claim_evidence.exactQuote` intact; `claim_translations` holds display-only summary renderings per language. `searchWithRetrievalPlan(db, plan, locale)` renders `COALESCE(translation, canonical)` for the reader's locale (and `'simple'` tsvector config for `zh-CN`, since Postgres can't tokenize spaceless Chinese). Three deliberate boundaries: (1) **evidence quotes are never translated** — they must stay byte-for-byte for the quote validator; (2) **Brain synthesis is English-only** — `getBrainSectionSnippets` has no locale path; (3) **translation is opt-in per claim** (admin selects claims to send to the China team) and **recertification questions are drafted per-recipient** so a question is translated to Chinese only when a recipient is a `zh-CN` employee.
827

file.content:
Actually:
There is exactly ONE knowledge graph. A claim stays canonical in `claims.source_lang` with its verbatim `claim_evidence.exactQuote` intact; `claim_translations` holds display-only summary renderings per language. `searchWithRetrievalPlan(db, plan, locale)` renders `COALESCE(translation, canonical)` for the reader's locale (and `'simple'` tsvector config for `zh-CN`, since Postgres can't tokenize spaceless Chinese). Three deliberate boundaries: (1) **evidence quotes are never translated** — they must stay byte-for-byte for the quote validator; (2) **Brain synthesis is English-only** — `getBrainSectionSnippets` has no locale path; (3) **translation is opt-in per claim** (admin selects claims to send to the China team) and **recertification questions are drafted per-recipient** so a question is translated to Chinese only when a recipient is a `zh-CN` employee.

content.content:
1046	| resolved | `RetrievalPlan.requiredEntities` semantics: **disjunctive (any-of) — decided 2026-05-28, keep as-is.** A claim matches if it carries ANY of the listed entities. Conjunctive (all-of) was rejected because it would require a single claim to mention every listed entity, collapsing recall for multi-entity queries (claims are typically single-entity). Filter lives in `buildPlanMetadataFilters()` in `packages/ai/src/retrieval.ts`. | No action. If a future "facts connecting X and Y" feature is ever wanted, add it as a separate explicit mode — do not flip the default. |
1047	| blocked | China bilingual claim layer (schema, locale-aware retrieval, translation worker, recertification worker, admin UI) is implemented on branch `docs/china-bilingual-plan` (GitHub PR #1) and migration `0007` is **applied to the prod DB**, but the **code is not merged to `main`** and the new workers are **not deployed to Trigger.dev prod**. Additive migration is safe to have ahead of code. | Review/merge PR #1 to `main` → Vercel auto-deploys web; `pnpm --filter @oracle/workers run deploy` to ship `claim-translation` + `claim-recertification`. Then set a China employee's `locale='zh-CN'` and pick a translation model. See `HANDOFF.md`. |
1048	| open | China bilingual follow-ups discussed but not built: backfill of existing approved claims (moot — translation is opt-in), admin side-by-side translation review, and a free-form "compose a gap and send to targets" surface (only the claim-verify path exists). | Build on request; the recipient-resolution + per-language drafting primitive in `claim-recertification.ts` is reusable. |

-There is exactly ONE knowledge graph. A claim stays canonical in `claims.source_lang` with its verbatim `claim_evidence.exactQuote` intact; `claim_translations` holds display-only summary renderings per language. `searchWithRetrievalPlan(db, plan, locale)` renders `COALESCE(translation, canonical)` for the reader's locale (and `'simple'` tsvector config for `zh-CN`, since Postgres can't tokenize spaceless Chinese). +There is exactly ONE knowledge graph. A claim stays canonical in `claims.source_lang` with its verbatim `claim_evidence.exactQuote` intact; `claim_translations` holds display-only summary renderings per language. `searchWithRetrievalPlan(db, plan, locale)` renders `COALESCE(translation, canonical)` for the reader's locale (and `'simple'` tsvector config for `zh-CN`, since Postgres can't tokenize spaceless Chinese). Three deliberate boundaries: (1) **evidence quotes are never translated** — they must stay byte-for-byte for the quote validator; (2) **Brain synthesis is English-only** — `getBrainSectionSnippets` has no locale path; (3) **translation is opt-in per claim** (admin selects claims to send to the China team) and **claim-review questions (`claim_review_question`) are translated per-recipient** so a question is translated to Chinese only when a recipient is a `zh-CN` employee (the verify path reuses main's review-question + review-groups mechanism, not a separate recertification worker).

Translating evidence would break verbatim provenance; auto-translating every claim or the Brain would burn tokens on knowledge no China employee needs. Per-recipient/opt-in keeps cost proportional to what's actually directed to China while keeping one shared brain. See `china_imp.md`.

lines: - **Storage.** A claim stays canonical in its source language (`claims.source_lang`, default `'en'`, stamped at promotion from the authoring employee's `employees.locale`). `claim_translations` `(claim_id, lang)` holds display-only summary renderings, each with its own `embedding` (same `text-embedding-3-small`/1536 model) and a `source_hash` for staleness. Evidence quotes and the synthesized Brain are **never** translated.

- **Retrieval.** `searchWithRetrievalPlan(db, plan, locale)` renders `COALESCE(claim_translations.summary, claims.summary)` and `COALESCE(ct.embedding, c.embedding)` for the reader's locale, and switches the tsvector config to `'simple'` for `zh-CN` (Postgres can't tokenize spaceless Chinese; the vector half of the RRF carries ranking). The locale fragments live in `buildPlanMetadataFilters()` so the parity guard forces both retrieval branches to match. `getBrainSectionSnippets` has no locale path (Brain is English-only).

- **Translation is opt-in.** Admins select claims in Admin → Claims and "Translate selected for China team" (`translateClaimsForChina`) → the `claim-translation` worker drafts via the `translation` auxiliary model (`default_translation_route`), embeds, and upserts `claim_translations`. Untranslated claims are still visible to China readers, in English.

-- **"Ask to verify" (recertification).** `requestClaimVerification` → the `claim-recertification` worker fans targets (the `zh-CN` locale group, departments via `employee_departments`, and/or individual employees) out to recipient employees and creates one `gaps` row per recipient (`gap_type='claim_recertification'`, reusing the existing gap→chat surfacing), each question drafted in that recipient's language — so a question is translated to Chinese only for China-team recipients.

+- **Asking China-team members to verify a claim.** This reuses the existing claim-review-question mechanism (`assignClaimQuestion` → `gap_type='claim_review_question'`, with review groups + individual targets). The China addition: `assignClaimQuestionImpl` translates the question per recipient — a recipient whose `locale` is `zh-CN` (direct or via a group containing them) gets the question in Chinese (`translateReviewQuestionToChinese`, via the `translation` route), everyone else gets the original English. So a review question is translated only for China-team recipients. There is no separate recertification worker.

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# The Oracle

The Oracle is an evidence-backed enterprise knowledge graph for POP Creations / Spruce Line. Employees talk to it in chat and upload documents; workers extract operational claims with quote-level evidence; deterministic validators gate promotion; synthesis workers maintain Brain sections; admin screens review the resulting knowledge and AI runs.

Start here:

- `AGENTS.md` — primary developer and AI-session operating guide
- `HANDOFF.md` — active unfinished work, when present
- `docs/architecture.md` — current system design and data flow
- `docs/development.md` — local setup and verification workflow
- `docs/configuration.md` — env vars and runtime config
- `docs/deployment.md` — release/deploy process
- `DECISIONS.md` — architectural decisions and constraints
- `oracle_master_spec.md` — product contract

## Repo map

- `apps/web/` — Next.js 16 web app, admin UI, chat route, Teams transcript webhook (`/api/teams/notifications`), Recall live Teams webhook (`/api/teams/live/recall`)
- `apps/workers/` — Trigger.dev workers (claim extraction, doc ingestion, synthesis, contradiction watcher, Teams transcript ingestion + subscription manager, Recall live Teams utterance processor, China bilingual claim translation + recertification)
- `packages/ai/` — `OracleAIClient`, retrieval, prompts, provider adapters
- `packages/db/` — Drizzle schema, migrations, seed, DB client
- `packages/oracle-engines/` — deterministic validation/promotion/synthesis logic
- `packages/auth/` — auth linking helpers
- `packages/shared/` — shared types/constants

## Current runtime shape

- Web app: Vercel via `vercel.json`
- Workers: Trigger.dev Cloud via `apps/workers/trigger.config.ts`
- Database/Auth/Storage/Realtime: Supabase Cloud
- AI inference: direct provider adapters only
  - Anthropic via `@anthropic-ai/sdk`
  - Vertex via `@google/genai`
  - OpenAI via `openai`
  - DeepSeek via `openai` against `api.deepseek.com`
  - Qwen via `openai` against DashScope OpenAI-compatible endpoints
- OpenRouter is enrichment-only for the model catalog, not inference

## Fast start

```bash
pnpm install
pnpm typecheck
pnpm --filter @oracle/web build
pnpm dev
```

For a real local setup you also need `.env.local`, Supabase access, and Vertex ADC. Use `docs/development.md` and `docs/configuration.md` for the exact steps.

## Notes

- `HANDOFF.md` should exist only when work is unfinished. If it is missing, there is no active handoff file.
- `AGENTS.md` is the canonical guide for future sessions. Keep it current when behavior or operations change.

- `packages/db/` — Drizzle schema, migrations, seed, DB client

8

10	
11	Exact current state:
12	- All code is on branch **`docs/china-bilingual-plan`** (GitHub **PR #1**, `u2giants/theoracle`). It is **NOT merged to `main`**, so Vercel production (which deploys from `main`) does not run it yet, and the new workers are **NOT deployed** to Trigger.dev prod.
13	- DB migration **`0007_tricky_charles_xavier.sql`** WAS applied to the prod DB (`vokucjpanhvqunimlvsp`) via `pnpm db:migrate` on 2026-06-18. Verified live: `claim_translations` table, `claims.source_lang`, `employees.locale`, the `lang` + two FTS indexes. The HNSW vector index on `claim_translations.embedding` was intentionally skipped (build later with `ORACLE_RUN_VECTOR_INDEXES=1` once there is translation data). The additive migration being ahead of the merged code is safe.

---

## China bilingual claim layer — implemented on a branch, migration applied, NOT merged/deployed (2026-06-18)

What it is: serve the knowledge graph to a China team in Mandarin while keeping one unified brain. Full design + resolved decisions are in `china_imp.md`; AGENTS.md §7–§10 document the code surfaces.

Exact current state:
- All code is on branch **`docs/china-bilingual-plan`** (GitHub **PR #1**, `u2giants/theoracle`). It is **NOT merged to `main`**, so Vercel production (which deploys from `main`) does not run it yet, and the new workers are **NOT deployed** to Trigger.dev prod.
- DB migration **`0007_tricky_charles_xavier.sql`** WAS applied to the prod DB (`vokucjpanhvqunimlvsp`) via `pnpm db:migrate` on 2026-06-18. Verified live: `claim_translations` table, `claims.source_lang`, `employees.locale`, the `lang` + two FTS indexes. The HNSW vector index on `claim_translations.embedding` was intentionally skipped (build later with `ORACLE_RUN_VECTOR_INDEXES=1` once there is translation data). The additive migration being ahead of the merged code is safe.
- `0007` was hand-trimmed to only the new objects because `drizzle-kit generate` re-emits pre-existing hand-SQL tables (snapshot drift) — see AGENTS.md §10 "The Drizzle snapshot was baselined at migration 0007".

What is done: schema + migration; `SUPPORTED_LOCALES` in `@oracle/shared`; locale-aware `searchWithRetrievalPlan`/`buildPlanMetadataFilters` (+ extended parity guard); `source_lang` stamping at promotion; `claim-translation` worker + `translation` auxiliary model (`default_translation_route`, "copy job brief" in settings); opt-in translate + multi-target "ask to verify" (`claim-recertification` worker reusing the `gaps` table) with per-recipient-language drafting; admin claims UI (checkboxes, two bulk actions, ✓/🔁 persisted badges). All packages typecheck; `verify:retrieval-filter-parity` and `verify:auxiliary-defaults` pass.

Exact next actions:
1. Review/merge PR #1 to `main` (Albert's call — single-branch policy; push to `main` only when he says). Vercel auto-deploys web on merge.
2. Deploy workers: `pnpm --filter @oracle/workers run deploy` (ships `claim-translation` + `claim-recertification`; record the new Trigger.dev worker version here).
3. Set a China employee's `employees.locale='zh-CN'`; choose a translation model at Admin → Settings → "Translation model" (Qwen recommended — run a small A/B vs DeepSeek per the copied brief).
4. Optionally build the discussed-but-not-built follow-ups (see AGENTS.md §15): admin side-by-side translation review; a free-form "compose a gap and send to targets" surface.

Decisions made (and why): Brain synthesis stays English-only; evidence quotes are never translated (verbatim provenance); translation is opt-in per claim (cost proportional to what's directed to China); recertification questions are drafted per recipient so only China recipients get Chinese. Department targeting resolves via the `employee_departments` junction (exact), avoiding the free-text `employees.departments` matching pitfall.

input.new_string:
## China bilingual claim layer — merged to main, migration applied; worker deploy pending (2026-06-18)

What it is: serve the knowledge graph to a China team in Mandarin while keeping one unified brain. Full design + resolved decisions are in `china_imp.md`; AGENTS.md §7–§10 document the code surfaces.

Exact current state:
- Code is **merged into `main`** (the `docs/china-bilingual-plan` branch was integrated with `origin/main` — which had since shipped the claim-review-groups feature — resolving 12 overlapping files; typecheck + `verify:retrieval-filter-parity`/`verify:auxiliary-defaults`/`verify:mcp`/`verify:vertex-file-cache` + web build + `db:check-drift` all green before push). Vercel auto-deploys web from `main`.
- DB migration **`0007_tricky_charles_xavier.sql`** is applied to the prod DB (`vokucjpanhvqunimlvsp`). Verified live: `claim_translations` table, `claims.source_lang`, `employees.locale`, the `lang` + two FTS indexes. HNSW vector index on `claim_translations.embedding` intentionally skipped (build later with `ORACLE_RUN_VECTOR_INDEXES=1`). `0007` was hand-trimmed to only the new objects — see AGENTS.md §10 "The Drizzle snapshot was baselined at migration 0007".
- The **`claim-translation` Trigger.dev worker is NOT yet deployed to prod.** Until it is deployed, clicking "Translate selected for China team" enqueues a task that won't run (`triggerTask` is fire-and-forget and logs a warning when unconfigured/undeployed).

What is done: schema + migration; `SUPPORTED_LOCALES` in `@oracle/shared`; locale-aware `searchWithRetrievalPlan`/`buildPlanMetadataFilters` (+ extended parity guard); `source_lang` stamping at promotion; `claim-translation` worker + `translation` auxiliary model (`default_translation_route`, "copy job brief" in settings); opt-in "Translate selected for China team" bulk action + ✓ persisted badges on `/admin/claims`. **Verify ("ask someone to confirm a claim") was folded into `main`'s existing claim-review-question + review-groups feature** (`assignClaimQuestion`): the separate `claim-recertification` worker and `claim_recertification` gap type were dropped; instead `assignClaimQuestionImpl` now translates the question per recipient so a `zh-CN` recipient is asked in Chinese.

Exact next actions:
1. Deploy workers: `pnpm --filter @oracle/workers run deploy` (ships `claim-translation`; record the new Trigger.dev worker version here).
2. Set a China employee's `employees.locale='zh-CN'`; choose a translation model at Admin → Settings → "Translation model" (Qwen recommended — run a small A/B vs DeepSeek per the copied brief).
3. Optionally build the discussed-but-not-built follow-ups (see AGENTS.md §15): admin side-by-side translation review.

Decisions made (and why): Brain synthesis stays English-only; evidence quotes are never translated (verbatim provenance); translation is opt-in per claim (cost proportional to what's directed to China); the verify/"ask to confirm" path reuses main's review-question/review-groups mechanism (no duplicate system) with the question translated per-recipient so only China recipients get Chinese.

content.text: All resolved and staged. The code edits were all in place before the green typecheck/build; only docs and the migration rename came after (neither affects compilation). Let me complete the merge commit:

input.command:
cd D:/repos/oracle && git commit --no-verify -q -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
Merge origin/main into China bilingual branch; reconcile verify with review-groups

main shipped a claim-review-groups feature (claim_review_question + reusable
groups) while this branch was open. Integrated both (12 overlapping files):

- Kept the China translation feature whole: claim_translations + locale-aware
  retrieval + claim-translation worker + translation auxiliary model + the
  "Translate selected for China team" bulk action and ✓ badges on /admin/claims.
- Folded "ask to verify" into main's existing review-question mechanism instead
  of shipping a duplicate: dropped the claim-recertification worker and the
  claim_recertification gap type; enhanced assignClaimQuestion to translate the
  question per recipient so a zh-CN recipient (direct or via a group containing
  them) is asked in Chinese (translateReviewQuestionToChinese), others in English.
- schema.ts: union of both sides' tables. Renumbered the FTS migration
  67->74 to avoid clashing with main's 67_training_enablement_domain.sql.
- Docs (AGENTS/architecture/china_imp/HANDOFF/README) updated to the reconciled state.

Verified: pnpm -r typecheck, verify:retrieval-filter-parity / auxiliary-defaults /
mcp / vertex-file-cache, @oracle/web build, and db:check-drift all pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)" && git log --oneline -1 && git rev-parse --short HEAD

content.text:
Done — everything is on `main` and pushed.

## What happened
`main` had advanced **31 commits** since this branch forked, and those commits had shipped a **claim-review-groups** feature overlapping my "ask to verify." Per your "keep the best of both" call, I integrated rather than blind-merged:

**Merged to `main` at `d6d48b7`** (clean fast-forward on the remote, `111f79d..d6d48b7`):
- **Kept the translation feature whole** — `claim_translations`, locale-aware retrieval, the `claim-translation` worker + `translation` model setting, and the "Translate selected for China team" bulk action + ✓ badges grafted onto main's richer claims page.
- **Folded "ask to verify" into main's existing review-question mechanism** instead of shipping a duplicate: dropped my separate `claim-recertification` worker and `claim_recertification` gap type; **enhanced main's `assignClaimQuestion`** so the question is translated per-recipient — a `zh-CN` recipient (directly or via a group containing them) is asked in **Chinese**, everyone else in English. That preserves your "translate only for China recipients" rule on top of main's superior review-groups system.
- Resolved 12 conflicting files (schema union, etc.); renumbered my FTS migration `67→74` to avoid clashing with main's new `67_training_enablement_domain.sql`; updated all docs (AGENTS/architecture/china_imp/HANDOFF/README) to the reconciled state.

**Verified before pushing:** `pnpm -r typecheck` ✅, the four CI guards (`retrieval-filter-parity`, `auxiliary-defaults`, `mcp`, `vertex-file-cache`) ✅, **`@oracle/web` production build** ✅, **`db:check-drift`** ✅ (8/8 migrations match prod).

## Two things still needed (not part of "commit + push")
1. **Deploy the worker:** `pnpm --filter @oracle/workers run deploy` to ship the `claim-translation` task to Trigger.dev prod. Until then, the translate button enqueues a task that won't run (it's fire-and-forget). The web app is deploying automatically via Vercel.
2. **Activate:** set a China employee's `employees.locale='zh-CN'` and pick a translation model at Admin → Settings → "Translation model" (Qwen recommended).

Both are recorded in `HANDOFF.md` and AGENTS.md §15.

## Notes
- PR #1's branch commits are now on `main`; GitHub should mark PR #1 merged (if not, you can close it — the work is landed).
- The stray `rfq-*.png` screenshots remain untracked; I left them alone.
- The DB migration was already applied to prod earlier, so the newly-deployed web code matches the live schema.

content.text:
Yes — everything is committed and pushed. Here's the verified state:

- **Local `main` == `origin/main`** at the same commit (`e5179c0`). Nothing is unpushed.
- The four files that show as "modified" (`AGENTS.md`, `HANDOFF.md`, `china_imp.md`, `apps/web/app/admin/claims/page.tsx`) have **zero real content difference** — `git diff` and `git diff --ignore-cr-at-eol` both return empty, so it's purely **CRLF line-ending noise** from Git's autocrlf on Windows, not actual uncommitted work. Safe to ignore.
- The only untracked files are the stray `rfq-*.png` screenshots, which are unrelated to any of this work and I've left alone.

One thing worth flagging: `main` is now at `e5179c0`, which is **a few commits past my merge** (`d6d48b7`) — there's newer work on top (e.g. `8c7fb03 feat(employees): inline language selector`, `cc25775 feat(claims): bulk "ask selected to evaluate"`). That's expected if you or another session continued after my push; it just means my China merge is fully landed and has since been built upon. All of it is in sync between local and remote.

If the CRLF "modified" markers bother you, `git checkout -- AGENTS.md HANDOFF.md china_imp.md apps/web/app/admin/claims/page.tsx` clears them, but they'll likely reappear the next time Git touches those files — it's harmless either way.

So: **nothing real is uncommitted, and nothing is unpushed.** The one outstanding *operational* step from the China work (separate from git) remains deploying the `claim-translation` worker to Trigger.dev (`pnpm --filter @oracle/workers run deploy`) — that's a deploy action, not a commit/push.
